skill.name: brenda-database
skill.description: Programmatic access to the BRENDA enzyme database via the SOAP API; use when you need kinetic constants (Km, kcat, Vmax), reaction equations, enzyme properties (pH/temperature optima, stability), or enzyme discovery by EC/substrate/product.
skill.summary: Programmatic access to the BRENDA enzyme database via the SOAP API for retrieving enzyme kinetic data, reaction equations, and enzyme properties.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 70 static findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The SHA256 password hashing at scripts/brenda_queries.py:38 is required by the BRENDA SOAP API specification and is not a security vulnerability. The external command findings correspond to legitimate code examples in markdown documentation (package installation, env var setup, script execution). The hardcoded URL points to the legitimate BRENDA enzyme database WSDL endpoint at brenda-enzymes.org. The hardcoded IP address findings are false alarms caused by the scanner confusing EC numbers (e.g., 1.1.1.1) with IP addresses. The heuristic CRITICAL combination finding (code+network+credential) is expected behavior for an authenticated API client connecting to a scientific database. This is a legitimate scientific research skill with no malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: SHA256 used for password hashing
low_findings.description: scripts/brenda_queries.py uses hashlib.sha256() to hash the BRENDA password before sending it in the SOAP API request. While SHA256 is not a dedicated password hashing function (no salt, single iteration), this format is required by the BRENDA SOAP API specification and is not a security vulnerability in the skill itself. The password hash is transmitted over HTTPS to the legitimate BRENDA endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SHA256 is confirmed in the source code at line 38. The usage matches the BRENDA API expected format, making it a false positive from a security standpoint but worth documenting.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for credentials
low_findings.description: The skill reads BRENDA_EMAIL and BRENDA_PASSWORD from environment variables for API authentication. This is standard and legitimate for API client credentials, but the credential names are well-scoped to the BRENDA service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence in source code and documentation. Credentials are properly scoped to the BRENDA-specific service with clear naming (BRENDA_EMAIL, BRENDA_PASSWORD).

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL to external API endpoint
low_findings.description: The skill contains a hardcoded URL pointing to the BRENDA enzyme database WSDL endpoint (brenda-enzymes.org). This is the legitimate and documented endpoint for the BRENDA SOAP API and is required for the skill to function.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL brenda-enzymes.org is confirmed as the official BRENDA database endpoint. The skill's documented purpose is to access this specific API.

content.user_title: Access the BRENDA Enzyme Database via AI
content.value_statement: Searching enzyme databases manually for kinetic constants, reaction data, and enzyme properties is slow and error-prone. This skill gives AI agents programmatic access to the BRENDA SOAP API to automatically retrieve enzyme data including Km, kcat, Vmax, reaction equations, pH optima, temperature optima, and inhibitor information.
actual_capabilities: Retrieve kinetic parameters (Km, kcat, Vmax) for specific enzymes by EC number and organism
Look up reaction equations and stoichiometry data for enzyme-catalyzed reactions
Search enzyme properties including pH optima, temperature optima, stability, inhibitors, and activators
Discover enzymes by searching with substrates, products, or EC numbers
Parse complex BRENDA SOAP response strings into structured Python dictionaries
Authenticate and query the BRENDA database programmatically via the SOAP API
limitations: Requires valid BRENDA database credentials (email and password) for API access
Only supports BRENDA enzyme data, not other biological or chemical databases
SOAP response parsing uses a simplified parser that may not handle all edge cases in BRENDA delimited string formats
Does not include local caching; repeated queries make new SOAP requests over the network

use_cases.title: Research enzyme kinetics for a scientific publication
use_cases.description: A biochemist researching enzyme kinetics can retrieve Km, kcat, and Vmax values for a specific enzyme across organisms to populate a research paper or create a reference database.

use_cases.title: Automate enzyme data retrieval in bioinformatics pipelines
use_cases.description: A bioinformatician can integrate BRENDA data into automated pipelines for enzyme annotation, metabolic modeling, or comparative genomics analysis across multiple species.

use_cases.title: Enzyme discovery by substrate or product for drug development
use_cases.description: A pharmaceutical researcher can find enzymes that act on specific substrates or produce target compounds for drug metabolism studies or metabolic engineering projects.

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve kinetic data for a known enzyme
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brenda-database skill to retrieve kinetic parameters for alcohol dehydrogenase (EC 1.1.1.1) from Homo sapiens. Return the Km, kcat, and Vmax values in a table with organism, parameter value, conditions, and reference.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs kinetic constants for a specific enzyme-organism combination for a paper or experiment design.

prompt_templates.title: Look up optimal reaction conditions
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the brenda-database skill, query the BRENDA database for the pH optimum and temperature optimum of the enzyme with EC number 1.1.1.1. Show the optimal range and any available stability data.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs to determine the best experimental conditions for assaying enzyme activity.

prompt_templates.title: Find enzymes that act on a specific substrate
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the brenda-database skill, search the BRENDA database for enzymes that use NADH as a substrate. For each result, provide the EC number, recommended name, organism, and the reaction equation.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs to identify all known enzymes that process a specific metabolite for pathway analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Build a comprehensive enzyme report for pathway analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the brenda-database skill, retrieve kinetic parameters (Km, kcat, Vmax) for enzymes in glycolysis from Homo sapiens: hexokinase (EC 2.7.1.1), phosphofructokinase (EC 2.7.1.11), and pyruvate kinase (EC 2.7.1.40). For each enzyme, include the reaction equation, optimal pH, optimal temperature, and known inhibitors. Compile results into a structured markdown report.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user needs to collect multi-enzyme data for metabolic pathway modeling or systems biology analysis.

output_examples.input: Retrieve kinetic parameters for alcohol dehydrogenase from Escherichia coli
output: - Data retrieved from BRENDA database via SOAP API
- Values shown are for the purified recombinant enzyme

output_examples.input: Search for optimal pH and temperature for EC 3.2.1.1 (alpha-amylase)

best_practices: Set BRENDA_EMAIL and BRENDA_PASSWORD environment variables before running queries to ensure authentication succeeds on the first attempt.
Validate EC numbers and organism names against BRENDA nomenclature before querying to avoid returning empty results from invalid identifiers.
Use specific organism names (e.g., Homo sapiens) rather than broad taxonomic groups to get more precise and relevant enzyme data.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode BRENDA credentials directly in scripts or commit them to version control; always use environment variables.
Do not fabricate enzyme kinetic data when the BRENDA database returns no results for a query; report the absence clearly.
Do not use this skill for databases other than BRENDA; the SOAP client and parsing logic are specific to the BRENDA API format.

faq.question: What is BRENDA?
faq.answer: BRENDA is a comprehensive enzyme database that provides kinetic, functional, and structural information about enzymes from all organisms.

faq.question: Do I need a BRENDA account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need valid BRENDA credentials (email and password) to access the SOAP API. Register at the BRENDA website to obtain access.

faq.question: What types of enzyme data can I retrieve?
faq.answer: You can retrieve kinetic constants (Km, kcat, Vmax), reaction equations, pH and temperature optima, stability data, inhibitors, and activators.

faq.question: How do I find an EC number for an enzyme?
faq.answer: EC numbers are enzyme classification codes such as 1.1.1.1 for alcohol dehydrogenase. You can search for them on the BRENDA website or in enzyme nomenclature databases.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without an internet connection?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires internet access to connect to the BRENDA SOAP API hosted at brenda-enzymes.org.

faq.question: What format does the BRENDA API return data in?
faq.answer: The BRENDA SOAP API returns data as delimited strings which are automatically parsed into structured Python dictionaries by the skill script.
